title: Skirmish in the Senate
content:
{{Top|fga|canon=Duel in the Galactic Senate}} {{Youmay|the [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]] between [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] and [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]|the [[Duel in the Senate]] fought between [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]]}} {{Duel |prev=*[[Infiltration of the Jedi Temple/Legends|Infiltration of the Jedi Temple]]{{Film|III}} *[[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|Mission to Mustafar]] |conc=[[Duel on Mustafar/Legends|Duel on Mustafar]] |next=[[Padmé Amidala's funeral/Legends|Funeral of Padmé Amidala]] |image=[[File:YodaPalpsduel.png]] |name=Skirmish in the Senate |conflict=[[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] |date=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] ([[Great ReSynchronization|16:5:23]]) |place=[[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] |result=*Sith victory **[[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] goes into [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] |side1=*[[Order of the Sith Lords]]/[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |side2=[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |side3= |side4= |forces1=*1 [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]]/[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] **[[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine/Darth Sidious]] |forces2=*1 [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]]/[[Master of the Order/Legends|Master of the Order]] **Yoda |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=2 [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Royal Guards]] (knocked unconscious) |casual2=None |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|At last—the Jedi are no more.''"
"''Not if anything to say about it, ''I'' have!|[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] and [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]|}} The '''Skirmish in the Senate''' was a confrontation that took place in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] between the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]. The two veterans of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] engaged in a ferocious [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]], with each making liberal use of their [[Force power/Legends|powers]]. Ultimately, the duel ended in a stalemate and Yoda chose to retreat. Afterwards, Yoda went into [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] while the Dark Lord rushed to [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] to rescue his apprentice, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who had been grievously injured by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] during their duel on Mustafar. ==Prelude== {{Quote|Destroy the Sith, we must.|Yoda to Obi-Wan Kenobi|}} The aftermath of the [[Showdown on Coruscant|duel]] between [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] and [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]—who was also [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] Darth Sidious—left the young [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] in turmoil—torn between his loyalty to the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and his wish to save his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], from [[Death/Legends|dying]] during childbirth, as he had seen in [[Force vision/Legends|visions]]. Palpatine, who had played the role of a mentor to the boy for many years, swayed Skywalker to his side by tempting him with the promise of [[Midi-chlorian manipulation|power]] to save his wife from death, which Darth Sidious claimed was only available to Anakin through the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. With no prospect of returning to the Jedi Order given his inadvertent role in Windu's death, he saw no other option for himself than to join the Dark Lord's cause. At that moment, Skywalker knelt before Sidious and was inducted into the [[Order of the Sith Lords]] as Darth Vader. Sidious then sent him to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] with the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] and [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|orders]] to kill all the Jedi within it. The Dark Lord also enacted [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], an emergency measure programmed into the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] fighting alongside Jedi in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] that commanded them to eliminate any and all Jedi around them. These two actions virtually eradicated the Jedi from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Before leaving the Jedi Temple, Vader had used the Temple's signal beacon to transmit a repeating message that any surviving Jedi needed to return to Coruscant. Vader hoped that this message would serve as a deadly lure to the lucky few Jedi who had managed to escape the Purge. Jedi Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], the Jedi [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]], both managed to survive Order 66 and discovered the message from the Temple. Suspecting that it was a trap for Jedi survivors, the two eventually [[Infiltration of the Jedi Temple/Legends|made their way]] back to the Jedi Temple with the intention of re-coding the Temple's message beacon. Upon arriving at the Temple, they encountered a clone trooper garrison that had been left there to execute any Jedi who returned as instructed by the beacon. The two Jedi eliminated the troopers and reconfigured the beacon to warn all surviving Jedi to go into hiding. On their way out, Kenobi and Yoda had looked through the Temple's security [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] footage and discovered that Skywalker, Kenobi's former [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], was commander of the slaughter.[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]] The two Jedi knew that they had to make an effort to destroy the two Sith Lords for the sake of the galaxy. Kenobi, overwhelmed with despair at seeing Anakin's betrayal, volunteered to take on Sidious while Yoda would fight Vader, as he was unable to bear the thought of striking down his "brother." However, Yoda knew that Kenobi was not powerful enough to fight Sidious, and said as much. He decided that Kenobi should go to attempt to kill Vader while Yoda engaged Sidious Given Yoda's strategy, he likely knew that if Sidious sensed that Vader was in danger, he would go immediately to aid him. Reluctanly agreeing, Kenobi left to find Vader on Mustafar while Yoda entered the [[Chancellery Secretariat/Legends|Chancellery Secretariat]] to confront Sidious. ==The duel== {{Quote|You will ''not'' stop me; Darth Vader will become ''more'' powerful than either of us.''"
"''Faith in your new apprentice, misplaced, may be—as is your faith in the dark side of the Force.|[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] and [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]|}} [[File:Sidslightning.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Sidious unleashes a barrage of [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] at [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]].]] Inside the [[Chancellor's working office/Legends|Galactic Senate Chamber Holding Office]] of the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]], the Grand Master confronted the Dark Lord of the Sith after [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force pushing]] his [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|guards]] against the wall, rendering them both unconscious. Yoda then mentioned that Palpatine, or rather, ''Darth Sidious'' had recently gained a new apprentice, while sarcastically mocking him to be the "Emperor". Sidious then remarked on his survival and when Yoda asked him if he was taken aback by it, Sidious told him contemptuously that he was blinded by ''his'' own hubris and that he would then be subject to the maximum might of the Dark Side. Sidious then blasted Yoda with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]]. Caught off-guard, Yoda was thrown across the chamber and slammed into a wall. As Sidious advanced on him Yoda quickly recovered and ignoring Sidious' [[Dun Möch/Legends|taunts]], retaliated with an equally powerful Force push that sent the Dark Lord flying over his desk in a heap. Yoda declared that Sidious' reign was over and not a moment too soon. Not wanting to take any risks at his moment of triumph, Sidious attempted to flee but Yoda blocked his way, igniting his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Realizing he would have no choice but to fight the Jedi Master blade-to-blade, Sidious ignited ''his'' own weapon after declaring that Darth Vader would become "far more powerful" than either of them, although Yoda responded by stating that that his "faith in Vader and the dark side may be misplaced". With nothing left to be said, the duel began. Their battle raged through the office, with neither opponent able to gain an advantage. As the fight moved to the [[Chancellor's Podium/Legends|Chancellor's Podium]], the platform rose through the retractable ceiling into the [[Grand Convocation Chamber]] itself. As the duel continued, Sidious found that his movements were being hampered by the small size of the Podium, while Yoda's [[Form IV/Legends|Ataru]] style and diminutive stature allowed him to easily jump around the Dark Lord. Yoda ultimately managed to dislodge Sidious's lightsaber out of his grip, disarming him. Sidious eventually took the high ground by jumping to one of the [[Repulsorpod/Legends|Senate pods]] above him. He then began to use the Force to throw waves of these pods at Yoda, but the Jedi Grandmaster used ''his'' own powers to dodge from one pod to another, avoiding Sidious's improvised projectiles. As Yoda leapt about, he attempted to re-engage Sidious in close combat, but the Dark Lord's attack was too intense for Yoda to close the distance. It was at that very moment, during a match between the embodiments of the light and the dark, that Yoda realized the Jedi had lost the war before it even started. In the last millennium, the Sith had [[Rule of Two/Legends|changed]] and adapted, becoming a new entity; while the Jedi Order, under his leadership, spent the same time merely training to re-fight the [[New Sith Wars|last war]]. [[File:YodaAtaru.png|thumb|right|200px|Yoda faces Sidious in the Grand Convocation Chamber.]] Shaking away his doubts, the tiny Jedi focused himself on an oncoming pod and sent it hurtling back at Sidious. As the pod flew back at Sidious, the Dark Lord realized that he had spread his own powers too thinly to counter the spinning projectile with the Force. He was instead forced to leap from his perch to a lower pod, relinquishing the tactical advantage. Yoda immediately saw his chance to re-engage Sidious at close quarters and leaped at the Sith Lord, landing on the edge of Sidious's pod and igniting his lightsaber. However, Sidious unleashed a blast of Force lightning at Yoda before he could strike, tearing [[Yoda's lightsaber/Legends|Yoda's lightsaber]] from his grasp, and causing Yoda to be forced to utilize [[tutaminis]] to absorb Sidious's lightning. Sidious advanced on Yoda, steadily strengthening the power of his lightning, while elevating his body in order to target his attack above Yoda's tutaminis defense, causing the latter to begin to falter. Yet suddenly, Yoda gained the strength necessary to start redirecting Sidious's lightning back at him, coalescing the energy into a blue orb in front of him, astounding Sidious who began to reel backwards with the strength of Yoda's counterattack. But just as Yoda appeared to be on the brink of succeeding, the combination of both of the Force master's powers caused an explosion of Force energy that hurled them both apart. Sidious was sent tumbling head over heels to the edge of the pod, but having been at its opposite end when the explosion transpired, he was not blasted clear of the pod and barely managed to grab one of its guardrails. Yoda however, had been standing at the very edge of the pod during the explosion, and weighed very little. As such, the blast sent Yoda flying all the way back to the Chancellor's Podium, situated high above the Senate floor with nothing in between but empty space. Yoda struck the Podium too hard to control his landing, and he rolled off the main portion to the edge where he desperately attempted to climb back up. His hold on the smooth edge failing, Yoda lost his grip and fell several stories to the floor. Yoda chose to retreat as Sidious cackled in triumph above him. If the galaxy had any hope of freedom from the Sith, Yoda realized that he needed to survive for that to happen. Yoda crawled through the ventilation shafts and eventually dropped into [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Prestor Organa's]] [[XJ-2 airspeeder|airspeeder]], escaping into [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]]. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Into exile, I must go; failed, I have.|[[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], to [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Prestor Organa]]|}} [[File:YodaEscapeShaft.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] escapes the Senate Rotunda through the ventilation shafts.]] [[Clone shock trooper/Legends|Shock troopers]] led by [[Clone Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Thire/Legends|Thire]] were not able to recover Yoda's body, and Mas Amedda was convinced that the Jedi Grand Master had escaped. Sidious assigned Thire and a squad of clones to search for the Jedi, even giving them permission to blow up the Senate building if they wished. Sidious himself had far more pressing matters to attend to, as his unease had progressed to a near-certainty that Vader was in mortal danger. On Mustafar, the Dark Lord found his apprentice near death due to the recent [[Duel on Mustafar/Legends|duel]] where Obi-Wan Kenobi dealt him what seemed mortal wounds. However, Sidious ultimately managed to save his apprentice. The magnitude of damage in the Senate Rotunda caught the eyes of the Senators. Rumors circulated in the Senate that Jedi Grand Master Yoda had led an attack on Palpatine himself, and that the ancient Jedi Master had been killed. Through [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] Senator [[Yarua/Legends|Yarua]], the rumor found its way to [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], including the ears of [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] and [[Tarfful/Legends|Tarfful]]. Eventually, the rumor reached [[Olee Starstone]] and her team of fugitive Jedi.''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' Having not been able to defeat Sidious, Yoda chose self-imposed exile to the swamp planet of [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]], where he would wait until Anakin's twins, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]], would be of age to turn their father back from the Dark Side. Years later, on Dagobah, Yoda would train Luke in the ways of the Force. Luke would ultimately go on to rescue his father from the dark side and inspire the latter to defeat Sidious, bringing balance to the Force. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Senate_Showdown.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Concept Art]] This lightsaber duel in the Senate Rotunda was created for the [[2005]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. It was unnamed in the film, but an official [[Hasbro]] [[Star Wars Miniatures]] pack that depicted the duel identified it as the Skirmish in the Senate.{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Saga Collection|packtype=Battle Pack|pack=Skirmish in the Senate|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/tsc06BPskirmish.asp}} According to ''[[The Making of Star Wars Revenge of the Sith]]'', [[Senate cam droid/Legends|Senate hovercams]] were meant to broadcast the duel across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] on the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]], but this idea was later dropped. In [[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|the novelization]], Yoda entered the office from another direction, so no Royal Guards attempted to stop him. Mas Amedda left the office before any exchange was made. Sidious' Force lightning was deflected by Yoda which blasted the guards into unconsciousness instead of himself being knocked across the room. Yoda then knocked Sidious to the floor with his physical body instead of a Force Push. At the end of the battle, the lightning energy ball did not explode. Sidious safely leaped to a nearby podium and Yoda followed. Sidious then turned around and blasted Yoda, who was still in the air, back against another podium who fell down to the bottom. Sidious was not knocked over by any blast, but he was described as "a very old, very tired man" after the epic battle. In the novelization, Yoda apparently does not lose his lightsaber in the battle, as he is described slicing his way through the ventilation shafts in his escape from the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]]. [[File:Yodakick.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Yoda knocks down Darth Sidious with a dropkick.]] In [[Star Wars: Episode III — Revenge of the Sith (comic series)|the comic]], the first half of the duel is a combination of scenes in the movie and the novelization. Yoda entered the office the way he did in the novel, but was blasted by Sidious as shown in the movie. The Red Guards and Mas Amedda mysteriously vanished after the battle started, and Yoda knocked Sidious down with his body as shown in the novel, though here in the shape of a flying dropkick. The duel in the comic ends after Yoda disarms Darth Sidious and gets blasted into the air.[[Star Wars: Episode III — Revenge of the Sith (comic series)|''Star Wars: Episode III — Revenge of the Sith'' comic adaptation]] In a DVD commentary, [[Rob Coleman]] stated that the Emperor's use of Senate pods as weapons was a metaphor for the fall of freedom and democracy brought about by Sidious' victory. There is a contradiction between two official sources about whether Yoda truly fainted or was merely playing weak when Sidious shot him with lightning and sent him flying across the office; the screenplay described Yoda as "stunned," while the junior novelization stated that Yoda faked it to surprise Darth Sidious. As the screenplay is G-canon, it is the official version. This is one of the few lightsaber duels in any of the films that does not result in any of the participants being injured in any way by a lightsaber. ==Appearances== *{{Film|III}} *[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]] {{Ab|Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (unabridged audiobook)}} *[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (junior
